next_steps: Obtain complete blood count with differential, inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP), and urinalysis
Initiate empiric intravenous antibiotic therapy for suspected meningococcemia
Perform echocardiogram to evaluate for coronary artery involvement
Consult pediatric rheumatology and infectious disease specialists
Consider skin biopsy if clinical diagnosis remains unclear
Monitor for hemodynamic instability and signs of organ involvement
Obtain complete blood count with differential, ESR, CRP, and renal/liver function panels
Perform urinalysis to screen for hematuria and proteinuria
Initiate empiric intravenous antibiotics pending blood and urine culture results to cover for Neisseria meningitidis
Consult pediatric rheumatology for evaluation of potential vasculitis
Obtain an echocardiogram to evaluate for cardiac involvement (e.g., Kawasaki disease or systemic inflammation)
Obtain COVID-19 PCR and serology to assess for MIS-C
Avoid initiation of systemic corticosteroids until infectious etiologies have been reasonably excluded
Monitor vital signs frequently for signs of hemodynamic instability
